title: nepeta grandiflora var. betonicifolia
intro: Nepeta grandiflora var. betonicifolia, also known as Himalayan catmint, is a perennial plant species belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and native to the Himalayas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Himalayan catmint include relief from anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory health. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
